questions.question: What is the chemical name of vitamin B-2?
questions.explanation: Riboflavin is a water-soluble vitamin that plays a key role in energy production, metabolism, and the maintenance of healthy skin, eyes, and nervous system. Other Information:- Thiamine: - The chemical name for vitamin B1, thiamine is a coenzyme that helps break down carbohydrates and produce neurotransmitters. Niacin: - The chemical name for vitamin B3, niacin is a water-soluble vitamin that includes nicotinic acid, nicotinamide, and related derivatives. Pantothenic acid: - The chemical name for vitamin B5, pantothenic acid helps the body convert food into fuel. Pyridoxine: - The chemical name for vitamin B6, pyridoxine helps maintain a healthy nervous system. Biotin: - The chemical name for vitamin B7, biotin is involved in many metabolic processes. Folate: - The chemical name for vitamin B9, folate helps with cell development, protein synthesis, DNA repair, and more. Cyanocobalamin: - The chemical name for vitamin B12, cyanocobalamin is a cobalt-containing compound produced by intestinal microorganisms.

questions.question: Oceanic Crust is made up of which type of rock?
questions.explanation: The oceanic crust is primarily made up of basaltic rock. Basalt is a dense, dark-colored, fine-grained igneous rock rich in iron [Fe] and magnesium [Mg] but low in silica [SiO₂]. It forms from the rapid cooling of lava at mid-ocean ridges, where new oceanic crust is continuously created through seafloor spreading. In contrast, the continental crust is mainly composed of granite, which is lighter, more silica-rich, and less dense than basalt.

questions.question: The market which has few dominant sellers and many buyers is known as which market?
questions.explanation: An oligopoly is a market structure where a few dominant sellers control the majority of the market, while there are many buyers. These sellers have significant market power, and their decisions influence each other. Examples include the automobile, airline, and telecom industries. Other options Explanation:- Monopoly: A single seller dominates the market. Monopolistic Competition: Many sellers offer differentiated products. Perfect Competition: Many sellers offer identical products.

questions.question: Who renounced the kaiser-e-hind on knowing about the Jallianwala Bagh massacre? > जलियाँवाला बाग हत्याकांड के बारे में जानने पर किसने कैसर-ए-हिंद का पद त्याग दिया?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi renounced the Kaiser-i-Hind medal in protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre [1919]. The Kaiser-i-Hind was a medal awarded by the British government for public service. Gandhi had received it in 1915 for his work in South Africa. However, after the brutal Jallianwala Bagh massacre on April 13, 1919, where British troops under General Dyer killed hundreds of unarmed Indians, Gandhi returned the medal as a symbolic act of resistance against British rule. > महात्मा गांधी ने जलियांवाला बाग हत्याकांड [1919] के विरोध में कैसर-ए-हिंद पदक त्याग दिया। कैसर-ए-हिंद ब्रिटिश सरकार द्वारा सार्वजनिक सेवा के लिए दिया जाने वाला पदक था। गांधी को यह पदक 1915 में दक्षिण अफ्रीका में उनके काम के लिए मिला था। हालाँकि, 13 अप्रैल, 1919 को हुए क्रूर जलियांवाला बाग हत्याकांड के बाद, जहाँ जनरल डायर के नेतृत्व में ब्रिटिश सैनिकों ने सैकड़ों निहत्थे भारतीयों को मार डाला था, गांधी ने ब्रिटिश शासन के खिलाफ प्रतिरोध के प्रतीकात्मक कार्य के रूप में पदक लौटा दिया।

questions.question: Which of the following war/battle is associated with the hanging of Mangal Pandey?
options: Indian Rebellion of 1857 >1857 का भारतीय विद्रोह
questions.explanation: Mangal Pandey was a sepoy in the 34th Bengal Native Infantry of the British East India Company. He played a key role in the beginning of the Indian Rebellion of 1857 by revolting against the use of the new Enfield rifle cartridges, which were rumoured to be greased with cow and pig fat. On March 29, 1857, he attacked British officers at Barrack pore and was later hanged on April 8, 1857. His actions sparked the larger rebellion against British rule. > मंगल पांडे ब्रिटिश ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी की 34वीं बंगाल नेटिव इन्फैंट्री में सिपाही थे। उन्होंने 1857 के भारतीय विद्रोह की शुरुआत में नई एनफील्ड राइफल कारतूसों के इस्तेमाल के खिलाफ विद्रोह करके महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाई थी, जिसके बारे में अफवाह थी कि उसमें गाय और सूअर की चर्बी लगी हुई थी। 29 मार्च, 1857 को उन्होंने बैरकपुर में ब्रिटिश अधिकारियों पर हमला किया और बाद में 8 अप्रैल, 1857 को उन्हें फांसी दे दी गई। उनके कार्यों ने ब्रिटिश शासन के खिलाफ बड़े विद्रोह को जन्म दिया।

questions.question: Which state won the 78th edition of the Santosh Trophy by defeating Kerala in the final?
questions.explanation: West Bengal won the 78th edition of the Santosh Trophy by defeating Kerala 1-0 in the final. The decisive goal was scored by Robi Hansda in added time. This victory marked West Bengal's 33rd Santosh Trophy title, extending their record as the most successful team in the tournament's history. The final match was held on December 31, 2024, at the GMC Balayogi Stadium in Hyderabad, Telangana. >पश्चिम बंगाल ने फाइनल में केरल को 1-0 से हराकर संतोष ट्रॉफी का 78वां संस्करण जीता। निर्णायक गोल रोबी हंसदा ने अतिरिक्त समय में किया। इस जीत ने पश्चिम बंगाल के 33वें संतोष ट्रॉफी खिताब को चिह्नित किया, जिसने टूर्नामेंट के इतिहास में सबसे सफल टीम के रूप में अपना रिकॉर्ड बढ़ाया। फाइनल मैच 31 दिसंबर, 2024 को तेलंगाना के हैदराबाद में जीएमसी बालयोगी स्टेडियम में खेला गया था।

questions.question: Which country will host the 28th Conference of Speakers and Presiding Officers of the Commonwealth [CSPOC] in 2026 ? > कौन सा देश 2026 में राष्ट्रमंडल के अध्यक्षों और पीठासीन अधिकारियों के 28वें सम्मेलन [सीएसपीओसी] की मेजबानी करेगा?
questions.explanation: India is set to host the 28th Conference of Speakers and Presiding Officers of Commonwealth Countries [CSPOC] in January 2026. This announcement was made by Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla during the CSPOC Standing Committee Meeting in Guernsey. The conference will focus on integrating artificial intelligence [AI] and social media into parliamentary processes, reflecting the growing influence of technology on legislative practices > भारत जनवरी 2026 में राष्ट्रमंडल देशों के अध्यक्षों और पीठासीन अधिकारियों के 28वें सम्मेलन [CSPOC] की मेज़बानी करने वाला है। यह घोषणा लोकसभा अध्यक्ष ओम बिरला ने ग्वेर्नसे में CSPOC की स्थायी समिति की बैठक के दौरान की। सम्मेलन में संसदीय प्रक्रियाओं में कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता [AI] और सोशल मीडिया को एकीकृत करने पर ध्यान केंद्रित किया जाएगा, जो विधायी प्रथाओं पर प्रौद्योगिकी के बढ़ते प्रभाव को दर्शाता है।

questions.question: Chad Laho is a dance-festival, primarily celebrated by which of the following tribes of Meghalaya? > चाड लाहो एक नृत्य-त्योहार है, जो मुख्य रूप से मेघालय की निम्नलिखित में से किस जनजाति द्वारा मनाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Laho Dance is a traditional folk dance of Meghalaya, primarily associated with the Jaintia tribe. This dance is an integral part of the Behdienkhlam festival, celebrated annually in July after the sowing period, to invoke divine blessings for a bountiful harvest. > लाहो नृत्य मेघालय का एक पारंपरिक लोक नृत्य है, जो मुख्य रूप से जैंतिया जनजाति से जुड़ा हुआ है। यह नृत्य बेहदीनखलम उत्सव का एक अभिन्न अंग है, जो बुवाई के बाद जुलाई में प्रतिवर्ष मनाया जाता है, ताकि भरपूर फसल के लिए ईश्वरीय आशीर्वाद प्राप्त किया जा सके।

questions.question: Coulomb’s law is applicable for _________. > कूलम्ब का नियम _________ पर लागू होता है।
options: Between two distributed charges >दो वितरित आवेशों के बीच
Between two point charges>दो बिंदु आवेशों के बीच
Both distributed charges and point charges >दोनों ने चार्ज और पॉइंट चार्ज वितरित किए
Neither point charges nor distributed charges>न तो बिंदु शुल्क और न ही वितरित शुल्क
questions.explanation: Coulomb's Law states that the electrostatic force between two point charges is directly proportional to the product of their magnitudes and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them. Coulomb's Law strictly applies to point charges rather than distributed charges, as distributed charges require integration methods to calculate the force. > कूलम्ब का नियम कहता है कि दो बिंदु आवेशों के बीच विद्युत-स्थैतिक बल उनके परिमाणों के गुणनफल के समानुपाती होता है तथा उनके बीच की दूरी के वर्ग के व्युत्क्रमानुपाती होता है। कूलम्ब का नियम वितरित आवेशों के बजाय बिंदु आवेशों पर सख्ती से लागू होता है, क्योंकि वितरित आवेशों को बल की गणना करने के लिए एकीकरण विधियों की आवश्यकता होती है।

questions.question: Gobinda Chandra Pal is an awardee of the Sangeet NatakAkademi Amrit Award for the year 2022. He contributed to the field of: > गोबिंद चंद्र पाल वर्ष 2022 के संगीत नाटक अकादमी अमृत पुरस्कार के विजेता हैं। उन्होंने किस क्षेत्र में योगदान दिया:
questions.explanation: Gobinda Chandra Pal is an awardee of the Sangeet Natak Akademi Amrit Award for the year 2022, recognized for his contributions to Odissi Dance [Gotipua]. >गोबिंद चंद्र पाल को ओडिसी नृत्य [गोटीपुआ] में उनके योगदान के लिए वर्ष 2022 के संगीत नाटक अकादमी अमृत पुरस्कार से सम्मानित किया गया है।

questions.question: The Swaran Singh committee had formulated ________-point code of fundamental duties. Done > स्वर्ण सिंह समिति ने मौलिक कर्तव्यों की ________-बिंदु संहिता तैयार की थी।
questions.explanation: The Swaran Singh Committee recommended adding 8 Fundamental Duties to the Constitution of India. However, when the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 was passed, the government included 10 Fundamental Duties under Article 51A. Later, in 2002, the 86th Amendment Act added one more duty, making the total 11 Fundamental Duties in the Indian Constitution. > स्वर्ण सिंह समिति ने भारतीय संविधान में 8 मौलिक कर्तव्य जोड़ने की सिफारिश की थी। हालाँकि, जब 42वाँ संशोधन अधिनियम, 1976 पारित हुआ, तो सरकार ने अनुच्छेद 51A के तहत 10 मौलिक कर्तव्य शामिल किए। बाद में, 2002 में, 86वें संशोधन अधिनियम ने एक और कर्तव्य जोड़ा, जिससे भारतीय संविधान में कुल 11 मौलिक कर्तव्य हो गए।

questions.question: Which type of unemployment is a short term Unemployment that occurs when people are changing jobs or entering the workforce?
questions.explanation: Frictional unemployment is a short-term type of unemployment that occurs when: 1. People are changing jobs 2. New individuals are entering the workforce [like recent graduates] It is a natural and unavoidable part of the labour market as people transition between jobs. Other Options Explanation: Disguised/Hidden unemployment: When more people are employed than necessary, often in agriculture, and their absence would not reduce output. Educated unemployment: When qualified individuals can't find jobs matching their education. Structural unemployment: Caused by long-term economic changes like automation or industry shifts.

questions.question: According to Mendeleev's Periodic Table, which element’s properties matched up remarkably well with eka-aluminium?
questions.explanation: Dmitri Mendeleev predicted the existence of an unknown element, which he called Eka-Aluminium, based on gaps in his periodic table. When Gallium was discovered in 1875 by Paul-Émile Lecoq de Boisbaudran, it closely matched Mendeleev’s predictions in terms of atomic mass, density, melting point, and chemical properties. > दिमित्री मेंडेलीव ने अपनी आवर्त सारणी में अंतराल के आधार पर एक अज्ञात तत्व के अस्तित्व की भविष्यवाणी की, जिसे उन्होंने एका-एल्यूमीनियम कहा। जब 1875 में पॉल-एमिल लेकोक डी बोइसबौड्रन द्वारा गैलियम की खोज की गई, तो यह परमाणु द्रव्यमान, घनत्व, गलनांक और रासायनिक गुणों के संदर्भ में मेंडेलीव की भविष्यवाणियों से काफी मेल खाता था।

questions.question: Kojagiri Poornima is a festival celebrated primarily in _______ on the full moon day of Ashwin month? > कोजागिरी पूर्णिमा अश्विन माह की पूर्णिमा के दिन मुख्य रूप से ______ में मनाया जाने वाला त्योहार है?
questions.explanation: Kojagiri Poornima is a popular festival primarily celebrated in Maharashtra on the full moon day of the Ashwin month. It is also known as "Sharad Purnima", and people celebrate it by staying awake at night, performing prayers, and consuming a special sweet dish called "Kheer ’’. >कोजागिरी पूर्णिमा एक लोकप्रिय त्यौहार है जो मुख्य रूप से महाराष्ट्र में अश्विन महीने की पूर्णिमा के दिन मनाया जाता है। इसे "शरद पूर्णिमा" के नाम से भी जाना जाता है और लोग रात में जागकर, पूजा-पाठ करके और "खीर" नामक एक विशेष मिठाई खाकर इसे मनाते हैं।

questions.question: Who among the following codify the composition of the Gurus into an authorized version i.e. Adi Granth Sahib? > निम्नलिखित में से किसने गुरुओं की रचना को एक अधिकृत संस्करण अर्थात् आदि ग्रंथ साहिब में संहिताबद्ध किया?
questions.explanation: The Adi Granth Sahib, also known as the Guru Granth Sahib, was compiled and authorized by Guru Arjan Dev, the fifth Sikh Guru, in 1604. Guru Arjan Dev compiled the Adi Granth Sahib and installed it at Harmandir Sahib [Golden Temple, Amritsar]. The Adi Granth included hymns of Guru Nanak Dev and the next four Gurus, along with compositions from Bhakti and Sufi saints like Kabir, Ravidas, and Namdev. >आदि ग्रंथ साहिब, जिसे गुरु ग्रंथ साहिब के नाम से भी जाना जाता है, को 1604 में पांचवें सिख गुरु, गुरु अर्जन देव द्वारा संकलित और अधिकृत किया गया था। गुरु अर्जन देव ने आदि ग्रंथ साहिब को संकलित किया और इसे हरमंदिर साहिब [स्वर्ण मंदिर, अमृतसर] में स्थापित किया। आदि ग्रंथ में गुरु नानक देव और अगले चार गुरुओं के भजनों के साथ-साथ कबीर, रविदास और नामदेव जैसे भक्ति और सूफी संतों की रचनाएँ शामिल थीं।

questions.question: The city of _______ was located on Khadir Beyt [also spelled as Bet] in the Rann of Kutch, where there was fresh water and fertile soil. ? > ________ शहर कच्छ के रण में खादिर बेत [जिसे बेट भी कहा जाता है] पर स्थित था, जहां ताज़ा पानी और उपजाऊ मिट्टी थी।
questions.explanation: Dholavira was an ancient city of the Harappan Civilization, located on the arid island of Khadir in the Kutch region of modern-day Gujarat, India. It is known for its advanced water management systems, including reservoirs and water channels, and its location provided access to both fresh water and fertile soil, making it an ideal settlement in the ancient times. > धोलावीरा हड़प्पा सभ्यता का एक प्राचीन शहर था, जो आधुनिक गुजरात, भारत के कच्छ क्षेत्र में खादिर के शुष्क द्वीप पर स्थित था। यह जलाशयों और जल चैनलों सहित अपनी उन्नत जल प्रबंधन प्रणालियों के लिए जाना जाता है, और इसका स्थान ताजे पानी और उपजाऊ मिट्टी दोनों तक पहुँच प्रदान करता है, जो इसे प्राचीन काल में एक आदर्श बस्ती बनाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following parts of the Indian Constitution deals with Amendment of the Constitution? >भारतीय संविधान का निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा भाग संविधान संशोधन से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Part XX [Article 368] empowers Parliament to amend the Constitution but sets out specific procedures and safeguards for doing so. These include varying levels of majority [simple, special] and, in some cases, ratification by state legislatures. It ensures that the Constitution remains adaptable to evolving circumstances while preserving its core principles. > भाग XX [अनुच्छेद 368] संसद को संविधान में संशोधन करने का अधिकार देता है, लेकिन ऐसा करने के लिए विशिष्ट प्रक्रियाएँ और सुरक्षा उपाय निर्धारित करता है। इनमें बहुमत के विभिन्न स्तर [सरल, विशेष] और, कुछ मामलों में, राज्य विधानसभाओं द्वारा अनुसमर्थन शामिल हैं। यह सुनिश्चित करता है कि संविधान अपने मूल सिद्धांतों को संरक्षित करते हुए बदलती परिस्थितियों के अनुकूल बना रहे।

questions.question: Which river flows into the Wular lake in the north and then into Baramulla? > कौन सी नदी उत्तर में वुलर झील और फिर बारामुल्ला में बहती है?
questions.explanation: The Jhelum River flows into Wular Lake in the north of India and then continues its journey towards Baramulla in Jammu and Kashmir. Wular Lake is one of the largest freshwater lakes in Asia, and the Jhelum River plays a significant role in feeding this lake. > झेलम नदी भारत के उत्तर में वुलर झील में बहती है और फिर जम्मू और कश्मीर के बारामुल्ला की ओर अपनी यात्रा जारी रखती है। वुलर झील एशिया की सबसे बड़ी मीठे पानी की झीलों में से एक है और झेलम नदी इस झील को पानी देने में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाती है।

questions.question: What is the elevation of Kanchenjunga?
questions.explanation: Kangchenjunga is the third-highest mountain in the world, with an elevation of 8,586 meters [28,169 feet] above sea level. It is located on the border between India and Nepal, with its main peak situated along the international boundary. The mountain is part of the Himalayas and lies in the Sikkim state of India and the eastern region of Nepal. > कंचनजंगा दुनिया का तीसरा सबसे ऊँचा पर्वत है, जिसकी समुद्र तल से ऊँचाई 8,586 मीटर [28,169 फ़ीट] है। यह भारत और नेपाल की सीमा पर स्थित है, जिसका मुख्य शिखर अंतरराष्ट्रीय सीमा पर स्थित है। यह पर्वत हिमालय का हिस्सा है और भारत के सिक्किम राज्य और नेपाल के पूर्वी क्षेत्र में स्थित है।

questions.question: Identify a muscle responsible for exit of waste material from the body? > शरीर से अपशिष्ट पदार्थ को बाहर निकालने के लिए जिम्मेदार मांसपेशी की पहचान करें?
questions.explanation: Sphincter muscles are responsible for controlling the exit of waste materials from the body. They are circular muscles that can contract or relax to regulate the passage of substances. In the case of waste elimination, the anal sphincter controls the release of feces from the body. >स्फिंक्टर मांसपेशियां शरीर से अपशिष्ट पदार्थों के बाहर निकलने को नियंत्रित करने के लिए जिम्मेदार होती हैं। वे गोलाकार मांसपेशियां हैं जो पदार्थों के मार्ग को विनियमित करने के लिए सिकुड़ या शिथिल हो सकती हैं। अपशिष्ट निष्कासन के मामले में, गुदा स्फिंक्टर शरीर से मल के निकलने को नियंत्रित करता है।

questions.question: Length of Indian border with Afghanistan? >अफगानिस्तान के साथ भारतीय सीमा की लंबाई?
questions.explanation: The border between India and Afghanistan is approximately 106 kilometers long. > भारत और अफगानिस्तान के बीच की सीमा लगभग 106 किलोमीटर लंबी है।

questions.question: Who discovered penicillin? > पेनिसिलिन की खोज किसने की?
questions.explanation: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928. He found that the mold Penicillium notatum had antibacterial properties, which led to the development of the first antibiotic, revolutionizing the treatment of bacterial infections. This discovery is considered one of the most important in medical history. >पेनिसिलिन की खोज अलेक्जेंडर फ्लेमिंग ने 1928 में की थी। उन्होंने पाया कि फफूंद पेनिसिलियम नोटेटम में जीवाणुरोधी गुण होते हैं, जिसके कारण पहली एंटीबायोटिक का विकास हुआ, जिसने जीवाणु संक्रमण के उपचार में क्रांति ला दी। इस खोज को चिकित्सा इतिहास में सबसे महत्वपूर्ण खोजों में से एक माना जाता है।

questions.question: Who is known as the first Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army? > भारतीय सेना के पहले कमांडर-इन-चीफ के रूप में किसे जाना जाता है?
options: General K. M. Cariappa>जनरल के. एम. करिअप्पा
Field Marshal Sam Manekshaw>फील्ड मार्शल सैम मानेक शॉ
questions.explanation: The correct answer is General K. M. Cariappa. He is known as the first Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army. He took over the position in 1949, after India gained independence. > सही उत्तर जनरल के.एम. करिअप्पा है। उन्हें भारतीय सेना के पहले कमांडर-इन-चीफ के रूप में जाना जाता है। उन्होंने भारत की स्वतंत्रता के बाद 1949 में यह पद संभाला था।

questions.question: Which object does not reflect more light? > कौनसी वस्तु अधिक प्रकाश परावर्तित नहीं करती?
options: Both A and B>A और B दोनों
questions.explanation: The correct answer is non-polished or unpolished surface. A polished or shining surface reflects more light because it is smooth and allows light to bounce off uniformly. questions.question: Amit completes a journey in 14 hours. He travels the first half of the journey at the speed of 27km/h and the second half of the it at 36km/h. The total distance of the journey is? > अमित एक यात्रा 14 घंटे में पूरी करता है। वह यात्रा का पहला आधा भाग 27 किमी/घंटा की गति से और दूसरा आधा भाग 36 किमी/घंटा की गति से तय करता है। यात्रा की कुल दूरी कितनी है?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706afaf00a5.65948187.gif) + ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706afbdeb23.96960171.gif) =1 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706afda6516.66886972.gif) =14 D=216km Total distance = 216×2=432 km Hence, option D is correct. Alternate Method: Since, distance in both cases is half; i.e. equal, therefore we may find here average speed of both given speeds. Average speed = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b0032b72.99745135.gif) = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b0156a07.93490298.gif) km/h [We may find average speed by using LCM Method too like Time & Work.] Hence, total journey covered in 14 hours = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b025e186.07465265.gif) ×14 = 432 km > ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b0367631.20060102.gif) + ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b04c0523.51825531.gif) =1 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b05b1431.60280924.gif) =14 D=216km कुल दूरी = 216×2=432 किमी इसलिए, विकल्प D सही है। वैकल्पिक विधि: चूंकि, दोनों मामलों में दूरी आधी है; यानी बराबर, इसलिए हम यहां दोनों दी गई गति की औसत गति पा सकते हैं। औसत गति = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b06c7740.94928901.gif) = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b0825d73.83426575.gif) किमी/घंटा [हम समय और कार्य जैसे LCM विधि का उपयोग करके भी औसत गति पा सकते हैं।] इसलिए, 14 घंटे में तय की गई कुल यात्रा = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699706b09855b1.50198429.gif) ×14 = 432 किमी

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: write beautiful poems. Explanation: • The subject of the relative clause is "who", which refers to "Raju" — a singular noun. • Since "Raju" is singular, the verb should also be singular. • The word "write" is a plural verb, which does not agree with the singular subject "Raju". • The correct singular verb form is "writes". Corrected Sentence: "Raju is the boy in my class who writes beautiful poems."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I know French and Spanish ___________ German.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Besides Explanation: The word "besides" is the most appropriate choice here. It is used to indicate addition or something in addition to the already mentioned items. In this case, you are saying that you know French and Spanish in addition to knowing German. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Besides – इसके अलावा – Used to add something additional to what has been mentioned. Example: "I know French and Spanish besides German." • Beside – के पास – Refers to something placed next to or at the side of something. Example: "She sat beside her friend during the movie." • Except – सिवाय – Refers to exclusion or something that is not included in the group. Example: "I know French and Spanish except German." [This would exclude German from the list, which is not what the sentence is conveying.] • Aside – किनारे – Refers to something placed or done to one side or in a different direction. Example: "He moved aside to let the others pass." Final Correct Sentence: "I know French and Spanish besides German."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I am much stronger than I was a year ago.
options: as I was the year before
that I were a year ago
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: No Improvement Explanation: • The original sentence "I am much stronger than I was a year ago" is grammatically correct and clear. • It uses the correct comparative structure "than I was" to compare strength between now and a past time [a year ago]. • There is subject-verb agreement, and the tense is appropriate for the context. Here are the options explained: • [A] as I was the year before – Incorrect; "as" is not suitable in a comparative structure where a difference is being expressed. "Than" is the correct word. • [B] than a year before – Incorrect; it is unclear what the comparison refers to — "a year before" what? It lacks clarity. • [C] that I were a year ago – Incorrect; "were" is wrong here. The correct verb form is "was" because the subject "I" takes "was" in the past simple tense.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: I remember to meet him five years ago.
All of you except Shiba have completed the project properly.
It is time you decide your next goal.
No sooner had they set out when it started to rain.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: All of you except Shiba have completed the project properly. Explanation: • The sentence is grammatically correct in terms of subject-verb agreement and sentence structure. • "All of you" is plural, so the plural verb "have completed" is appropriate. • The phrase "except Shiba" does not affect the verb agreement with "All of you." Why the other options are incorrect: [A] I remember to meet him five years ago. Incorrect usage of "to meet" — when referring to a past memory, we use the gerund ["meeting"]. Correct version: I remember meeting him five years ago. [C] It is time you decide your next goal. "It is time" should be followed by the past simple tense even when referring to the present or future [subjunctive mood]. Correct version: It is time you decided your next goal. [D] No sooner had they set out when it started to rain. After "No sooner," we must use "than", not "when". Correct version: No sooner had they set out than it started to rain.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. She's really cut in for teaching as she's patient and kind.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cut out Explanation: The phrasal verb "cut out" is the most appropriate choice here. The phrase "cut out for" means to be naturally suited or well-equipped for a particular job or role. In this sentence, it suggests that the person has the qualities [like patience and kindness] that make her suitable for teaching. Here are the meanings and examples of all the phrasal verbs: • Cut out – उपयुक्त होना – To be naturally suited for something. Example: "He’s cut out for leadership roles." • Cut down – कम करना – To reduce the amount or size of something. Example: "She’s trying to cut down on fast food." • Cut off – अलग कर देना / रोक देना – To stop or interrupt suddenly, or to disconnect. Example: "The storm cut off electricity in the village." • Cut in – बीच में बोलना / टोकना – To interrupt or join suddenly. Example: "He rudely cut in while I was speaking." Corrected Sentence: "She’s really cut out for teaching as she’s patient and kind."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. He drove _____ the office early in the morning.
questions.explanation: Correct answer: To Explanation: • The verb "drove" is commonly followed by the preposition "to" when talking about traveling to a specific destination. • "To" shows movement in the direction of a specific place — in this case, the office. Examples: • She drove to the supermarket yesterday. • They drove to the beach for the weekend. Corrected Sentence: He drove to the office early in the morning.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: Whole the chapter of this grammar
book is full of errors this shows
the carelessness of the proof reader.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Whole the chapter of this grammar Explanation: • The phrase "Whole the chapter" is grammatically incorrect. • The correct order is: "The whole chapter" — the definite article "the" should come before the adjective "whole". CONCEPT: • When using adjectives like whole, entire, or complete, they usually follow "the": • ✅ The whole chapter is full of errors. • ❌ Whole the chapter is full of errors. Corrected Sentence: The whole chapter of this grammar book is full of errors; this shows the carelessness of the proofreader.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. For example, some people think visualisation works best for them. B. Some others go with affirmations, where they repeat positive statements to reinforce belief in themselves. C. There are hundreds of manifesting rituals and ways, and different ones suit people differently. D. They create a clear mental image of their goals, and do it repeatedly, improving constantly.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: CADB Explanation: • C introduces the topic of manifesting rituals, explaining that there are many methods, and they suit people in different ways. • A provides a specific example of one of these methods—visualisation—and how some people find it effective. • D further explains the visualisation technique, describing how people create clear mental images of their goals. • B offers another method, affirmations, where people repeat positive statements to reinforce belief in themselves. Final Paragraph: There are hundreds of manifesting rituals and ways, and different ones suit people differently. For example, some people think visualisation works best for them. They create a clear mental image of their goals, and do it repeatedly, improving constantly. Some others go with affirmations, where they repeat positive statements to reinforce belief in themselves.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Where did he send the letter?
options: Where is the letter sent by him?
Where has the letter been sent by him?
Where can the letter be sent by him?
Where was the letter sent by him?
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence "Where did he send the letter?" is in the active voice, with "he" as the subject, "send" as the verb, and "the letter" as the object. • To change it to the passive voice, we need to make the object ["the letter"] the subject of the sentence. • The verb "send" becomes "was sent" in the past tense, and we include "by him" to indicate the agent [the one who performed the action]. • The correct structure for passive voice in past tense is "was" + past participle. Final Passive Voice Sentence: Where was the letter sent by him?

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. She said, "Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi March in 1930."
options: She said that Mahatma Gandhi had led the Dandi March in 1930.
She said that Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi March in 1930.
She said Mahatma Gandhi was leading the Dandi March in 1930.
She said that Mahatma Gandhi would lead the Dandi March in 1930.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The reporting verb is "said", which is in the past tense. • However, the statement refers to a historical fact — something universally or generally known to be true. • In such cases, the verb inside the quotation marks does not change tense during narration change. • So, "led" remains "led" in the indirect speech because it refers to a well-known past event that doesn't require back shifting to "had led." Final Reported Sentence: She said that Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi March in 1930.

Rampant deforestation, driven by infrastructure development, mining, and tourism, has precipitated the fragmentation of habitats, threatening endemic flora and fauna with imminent ____1____. Despite comprehensive legal safeguards, enforcement mechanisms remain inadequate, allowing illicit logging and habitat encroachment to persist unabated. Environmental advocates call for urgent, decisive interventions before the damage becomes irrevocable and ____2____. While public consciousness regarding conservation has burgeoned among urban populations and youth, the indispensable role of indigenous communities as custodians of biodiversity is increasingly acknowledged. Their active engagement is vital ____3____ sustainable conservation strategies. The realization of effective ecological preservation hinges on robust legal frameworks complemented by meaningful community ____4____ and stewardship. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation- The sentence is discussing the urgent need for conservation efforts to prevent environmental damage from becoming permanent. The word "irrevocable" is already used, but we're looking for a word that complements it and emphasizes the importance of taking action. Options: - Indelible: This word means "lasting or persistent" and typically refers to marks or impressions that cannot be erased. While it conveys a sense of permanence, it doesn't quite fit the context of environmental damage. - Irrevocable: This word means "unable to be changed or reversed". Using it again would be redundant, and the sentence already mentions that the damage should be prevented from becoming irrevocable. - Immutable: This word means "unchanging or unchangeable". While it conveys a sense of permanence, it's not the best fit in this context, as it doesn't necessarily imply a negative connotation. - Irreversible: This word means "unable to be reversed or turned back". In the context of environmental damage, it implies that the harm would be permanent and impossible to undo. Given the context, the most suitable option is irreversible. It emphasizes the importance of taking action to prevent environmental damage from becoming permanent and irreparable.
